source_quote: White LED D11 absent; System Controller (LPC) unresponsive to SYSCTL UART commands. Likely cause: Always-on 3.3V regulator U14 failed or its output is shorted, starving the LPC11U24 (U18) of its supply rail LPC_VCC. Measure J22 pin 15 for 3.3 V; if absent, probe U14 output in diode-mode for short.

evidence.reasoning: U14 named literally; bridge maps to U14 in graph.

evidence.source_quote_substring: starving the LPC11U24 (U18) of its supply rail LPC_VCC
evidence.reasoning: U14 is source_refdes of LPC_VCC per the graph; failure kills LPC_VCC.
U18 powered by LPC_VCC; loss of rail makes U18 non-functional. D11 also indicates LPC_VCC and goes dark.
D11 indicator powered by LPC_VCC; goes dark when U14 is dead.

source_quote: Always-on 3.3V regulator U14 failed or its output is shorted, starving the LPC11U24 (U18) of its supply rail LPC_VCC. if U14 output is shorted, isolate downstream load (U18 et al.) before replacing.

evidence.reasoning: U14 literal; bridge maps to U14.
U14 sources LPC_VCC per graph; shorted output collapses the rail.
U18 loses supply when LPC_VCC is shorted to GND.
